title: Disabling CSRF protections is security-sensitive
description: Disabling CSRF protections is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalsecuritySecurity Hotspotcweexpress.jsA cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attack occurs when a trusted user of a web application can be forced, by an attacker, to perform sensitive actions that he didn’t intend, such as updating his profile or sending a message, more generally anything that can change the state of the application.The attacker can trick the user/victim to click on a link, corresponding to the privileged action, or to visit a malicious web site that embeds a hidden web request and as web browsers automatically include cookies, the actions can be authenticated and sensitive.Ask Yourself WhetherThe web application uses cookies to authenticate users.There exist sensitive operations in the we
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Express routes should use CSRF protection middleware
